skill.name: longbridge-intel
skill.description: Longbridge Intel helps agents build market intelligence reports using Longbridge CLI, MCP fallback, and selective web research. It covers screeners, rankings, movers, ETFs, sectors, catalysts, event strategies, supply chains, and ARK-style innovation analysis.
skill.summary: Create structured Longbridge market intelligence reports across stocks, ETFs, sectors, catalysts, and themes.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external-command and weak-crypto indicators, but the high weak-crypto findings are false positives from finance terminology and markdown formulas. The confirmed risks are legitimate external Longbridge CLI execution and network-backed MCP or WebSearch fallback for market data. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential theft, obfuscation, persistence, or destructive filesystem behavior.

medium_findings.title: External Longbridge CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill repeatedly instructs the agent to run Longbridge CLI commands and command help checks. This is expected for the skill, but it executes local external commands and depends on the installed longbridge-terminal binary, so marketplace users should treat it as a command-executing research skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain explicit longbridge command invocations in markdown code blocks and workflow text. The commands are domain-specific and not malicious, but the external-command behavior is real.

medium_findings.title: Network-Backed Market Data and WebSearch Fallback
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill directs agents to use an MCP endpoint and WebSearch when Longbridge data is incomplete. This is legitimate for market research, but it can disclose queried symbols, sectors, and research topics to external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The MCP URL and WebSearch fallback are explicitly documented. I did not find evidence of secret exfiltration, but network disclosure of user research topics is inherent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are Finance-Term False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The high-risk weak cryptography detections map to financial words and formulas such as ARK, market cap, cash-flow links, and market microstructure metrics. I found no hashing, encryption, signature, or password-storage implementation in the cited markdown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows finance analysis prose and formulas, not cryptographic APIs or weak algorithms. The detector likely matched short substrings such as cap, cash, or ARK.

low_findings.title: Dead Reference Links Reduce Reliability
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: Some reference files point to supporting files that are not present in this package, such as scoring and output templates. This is not a direct security issue, but it can make the skill produce incomplete or inconsistent guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines reference files not listed in the scanned file tree. This affects completeness rather than indicating malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runs External CLI Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses shell-facing Longbridge commands for quotes, filings, financial reports, kline data, constituents, capital flow, and other market data. Commands use placeholder symbols and fixed subcommands rather than arbitrary user-provided shell fragments, which lowers injection risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code blocks contain concrete external command examples. The commands are constrained to Longbridge market-data operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Uses External Data Services
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents MCP and WebSearch fallbacks for market data, TAM figures, learning rates, and qualitative research. This is expected functionality but should be disclosed because user research topics may leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicitly described, including a Longbridge MCP URL and WebSearch query patterns. No suspicious exfiltration endpoint was found.

content.value_statement: Market research often requires many data pulls and consistent report structure. Longbridge Intel gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code repeatable workflows for screeners, ETFs, sectors, catalysts, and thematic analysis.
actual_capabilities: Routes market questions to focused reference workflows for screeners, rankings, movers, ETFs, sectors, catalysts, and supply chains.
Guides agents to fetch Longbridge quotes, financial reports, filings, news, kline data, constituents, and capital-flow data.
Defines structured output formats for morning briefs, ETF reviews, event briefs, sector rotation snapshots, and industry overviews.
Supports Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English response styles.
Includes disclaimers and guardrails for investment research outputs.
limitations: Requires longbridge-terminal, MCP access, or web search for live data retrieval.
Does not execute trades or provide personalized financial advice.
Some internal reference links named in the prose are not included in the package.
Market data quality depends on Longbridge coverage and the user environment.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Daily Market Brief
use_cases.description: Generate a concise morning view that combines movers, watchlist catalysts, sector changes, and upcoming events.

use_cases.title: Compare ETF Allocation Choices
use_cases.description: Review ETF holdings, tracking error, liquidity, premium or discount, and portfolio overlap before deeper due diligence.

use_cases.title: Map Sector and Supply-Chain Themes
use_cases.description: Build industry overviews that identify key companies, valuation context, bottlenecks, and thematic catalysts.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Market Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Longbridge morning brief for the US market. Include top movers, relevant news, sector signals, and a short watchlist.

prompt_templates.title: ETF Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze QQQ.US with the ETF framework. Cover product profile, tracking error, liquidity, premium or discount, holdings, and allocation fit.

prompt_templates.title: Sector Rotation Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a sector rotation view for A-share industries using a 20-day momentum window. Rank leaders and laggards with capital-flow context.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Innovation Thesis
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate TSLA.US with the ARK-style innovation framework. Apply suitability gating, TAM ranges, cost-curve assumptions, scenarios, risks, and source reconciliation.

output_examples.input: Give me a morning brief for Hong Kong stocks.
output_examples.output: A market brief with the nearest market session context, major index moves, top unusual movers, watchlist catalysts, sector signals, and an investment disclaimer.

output_examples.input: Compare SPY.US and QQQ.US for allocation fit.
output_examples.output: An ETF comparison covering market exposure, liquidity, tracking behavior, holdings concentration, overlap, and practical allocation considerations.
A supply-chain report that separates upstream, midstream, and downstream tiers, then highlights key companies, bottlenecks, margins, catalysts, and risks.

best_practices: Confirm the market, ticker format, and time window before requesting data.
Use Longbridge data first and label any WebSearch-sourced figures clearly.
Treat outputs as research context and verify important figures before making decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for personalized buy, sell, or position-sizing instructions.
Do not hide missing data or replace unavailable figures with unsupported estimates.
Do not run broad scans when the user only asked for one ticker or one sector.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Longbridge account?
faq.answer: The package says public CLI commands do not require login, but some data may still depend on local Longbridge access.

faq.question: Can it trade stocks for me?
faq.answer: No. It creates research workflows and reports only. It does not place orders.

faq.question: Which markets does it cover?
faq.answer: The references mention US, Hong Kong, A-share, and Singapore market contexts, depending on Longbridge data availability.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security rating?
faq.answer: The skill asks agents to run Longbridge CLI commands and may use MCP or WebSearch for data. That behavior should be disclosed.

faq.question: Can it work in English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports English, Simplified Chinese, and Traditional Chinese response styles.

faq.question: Is the ARK-style analysis affiliated with ARK Invest?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that it is independent and not affiliated with or endorsed by ARK Invest.
